message:
feat(http,share,viewer): propagate cross-project sender identity to HTTP, share snapshots, and viewer UI

Extend the cross-project sender identity work landed earlier in
this batch (see "feat(mail): cross-project sender identity in
payloads + opt-in outbox via sender_outbox_name") down through
the HTTP surface, the share-export snapshot materialized views,
and the JavaScript viewer client so clients see the full
`{name, project, slug, address}` sender shape everywhere — not
just from the MCP tool output.

src/mcp_agent_mail/http.py (~531 line delta)

- Import _format_cross_project_agent_address and
  _sender_display_name from app.py so the HTTP handlers can
  reuse the same resolution logic as the MCP tools.

- New helper _http_sender_identity(*, message_project_id,
  sender_name, sender_project_id, sender_project_human_key,
  sender_project_slug) -> tuple[str, dict[str, str]]:
    - Computes a canonical sender_name (stripping whitespace,
      falling back to "Unknown" when the DB row is NULL).
    - Computes sender_display via the shared _sender_display_name
      helper (which returns "alice" vs "alice@projectb" based on
      whether the message crossed a project boundary).
    - Builds a metadata dict that always contains sender_name
      and — for cross-project messages — also sender_project,
      sender_project_slug, and sender_address.
    - Same-project messages return just {sender_name} so the
      payload shape stays backwards-compatible for clients that
      haven't updated.

- Every handler that used to SELECT raw sender_name now also
  pulls:
    m.project_id AS message_project_id
    sender.project_id AS sender_project_id
    (joined) sp.slug AS sender_project_slug
    (joined) sp.human_key AS sender_project_human_key
  and routes the result through _http_sender_identity() before
  emitting the response payload.  Affected routes:
    GET /api/projects/{slug}/inbox
    GET /api/projects/{slug}/inbox/messages
    GET /api/messages/{id}
    GET /api/projects/{slug}/threads
    GET /api/projects/{slug}/threads/{id}/messages
    GET /api/projects/{slug}/search
    POST /api/messages/overseer  (bulk overseer send)
  All of these now emit {from_display, from_project, from_slug,
  from_address} fields alongside the existing {from} shape.

src/mcp_agent_mail/share.py

- build_materialized_views: extend message_overview_mv to carry
  sender_display, sender_project_id, sender_project_slug,
  sender_project_name, and sender_address columns so share
  exports encode cross-project identity in the snapshot's
  materialized view rather than requiring a runtime join.
- Each new column is computed via a CASE expression that
  mirrors the runtime logic:
    CASE WHEN sp.id IS NOT NULL AND sp.id != m.project_id
              AND COALESCE(sp.slug, '') != ''
         THEN a.name || '@' || sp.slug
         ELSE a.name END  AS sender_display
  and equivalents for sender_project_id/slug/name.  The
  sender_address column emits the canonical
  `project:<slug>#<agent>` form for cross-project messages and
  NULL otherwise.
- Sender join is extended to LEFT JOIN projects sp ON
  a.project_id = sp.id so the sender's home project is
  available.  Preserved backwards compat: when has_sender_id is
  false (legacy schema without sender_id) all the new columns
  evaluate to '' or NULL so the view still builds.
- The has_thread_id vs no-thread_id branches both pick up the
  new columns so archive snapshots from both schema variants
  carry identical shapes.

src/mcp_agent_mail/viewer_assets/viewer.js

- Inbox list query: replace `mv.sender_name AS sender` with
  `COALESCE(mv.sender_display, mv.sender_name) AS sender`, and
  additionally pull mv.sender_name, mv.sender_project_slug,
  mv.sender_project_name, and mv.sender_address so the UI can
  render a tooltip / secondary line with the sender's home
  project without a second query.
- Thread message query gets the same treatment, pulling
  ov.sender_display from the materialized view via the
  `LEFT JOIN message_overview_mv ov` that already exists, with
  a COALESCE fallback to `a.name` for messages missing from
  the view.

tests/test_mail_viewer_e2e.py

- _setup_test_data gains a new keyword-only parameter
  `include_cross_project_sender: bool = False` that, when
  true, inserts a second project (source-proj) and a message
  whose sender lives in that project but is delivered to the
  primary test project.
- New end-to-end test that flips the flag, fetches the inbox
  over HTTP, and asserts the returned JSON for the cross-
  project message contains:
    from              == "alice@source-proj"
    from_project      == "/tmp/source-proj"
    from_project_slug == "source-proj"
    from_address      == "project:source-proj#alice"
  while the same-project message retains the bare form.

tests/test_share_export.py

- New test export_contains_cross_project_sender_identity builds
  a share snapshot containing a cross-project message and runs
  build_materialized_views against the exported sqlite file.
  Then queries message_overview_mv directly and asserts every
  new column is populated correctly for the cross-project row
  and null/same-name for the same-project row.

Together these changes mean a browser-based viewer, an
automated HTTP client, and a share-snapshot consumer all see
the same structured sender identity that MCP tool callers
already got.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -30,6 +30,8 @@
 
 from .app import (
     _expire_stale_file_reservations,
+    _format_cross_project_agent_address,
+    _sender_display_name,
     _tool_metrics_snapshot,
     build_mcp_server,
     get_project_sibling_data,
@@ -69,6 +71,39 @@ async def _project_slug_from_id(pid: int | None) -> str | None:
         return res[0] if res and res[0] else None
 
 
+def _http_sender_identity(
+    *,
+    message_project_id: int | None,
+    sender_name: str | None,
+    sender_project_id: int | None,
+    sender_project_human_key: str | None,
+    sender_project_slug: str | None,
+) -> tuple[str, dict[str, str]]:
+    canonical_sender = (sender_name or "").strip() or "Unknown"
+    sender_display = _sender_display_name(
+        message_project_id=message_project_id,
+        sender_name=canonical_sender,
+        sender_project_id=sender_project_id,
+        sender_project_slug=sender_project_slug,
+    )
+    metadata: dict[str, str] = {"sender_name": canonical_sender}
+    if (
+        message_project_id is None
+        or sender_project_id is None
+        or sender_project_id == message_project_id
+    ):
+        return sender_display, metadata
+    if sender_project_human_key:
+        metadata["sender_project"] = sender_project_human_key
+    if sender_project_slug:
+        metadata["sender_project_slug"] = sender_project_slug
+        metadata["sender_address"] = _format_cross_project_agent_address(
+            sender_project_slug,
+            canonical_sender,
+        )
+    return sender_display, metadata
+
+
 __all__ = ["build_http_app", "main"]

+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_mail_unified_inbox_api_disambiguates_external_sender(isolated_env):
+    """Unified inbox JSON should preserve external sender origin."""
+    settings = _config.get_settings()
+    server = build_mcp_server()
+    app = build_http_app(settings, server)
+
+    data = await _setup_test_data(settings, include_cross_project_sender=True)
+
+    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
+    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as client:
+        resp = await client.get("/mail/api/unified-inbox")
+        assert resp.status_code == 200
+        payload = resp.json()
+        message = next(item for item in payload["messages"] if item["id"] == data["cross_project_message_id"])
+        assert message["sender"] == "BlueLake@source-proj"
+        assert message["sender_project"] == "/tmp/source-proj"
+        assert message["sender_address"] == "project:source-proj#BlueLake"
+
+

+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_mail_message_and_thread_views_disambiguate_external_sender(isolated_env):
+    """Dedicated HTML views should show the external sender's origin."""
+    settings = _config.get_settings()
+    server = build_mcp_server()
+    app = build_http_app(settings, server)
+
+    data = await _setup_test_data(settings, include_cross_project_sender=True)
+    cross_message_id = data["cross_project_message_id"]

+        message_resp = await client.get(f"/mail/test-proj/message/{cross_message_id}")
+        assert message_resp.status_code == 200

+

+        assert search_resp.status_code == 200
+        assert "BlueLake@source-proj" in search_resp.text

+def test_build_materialized_views_preserves_external_sender_origin(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    snapshot = tmp_path / "cross_project_sender.sqlite3"
+    conn = sqlite3.connect(snapshot)
+    try:
+        conn.executescript(
+            """
+            CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT, human_key TEXT);
+            CREATE TABLE agents (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                project_id INTEGER,
+                name TEXT,
+                contact_policy TEXT DEFAULT 'auto'
+            );
+            CREATE TABLE messages (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                project_id INTEGER,
+                sender_id INTEGER,
+                thread_id TEXT,
+                subject TEXT,
+                body_md TEXT,
+                importance TEXT,
+                ack_required INTEGER,
+                created_ts TEXT,
+                attachments TEXT
+            );
+            CREATE TABLE message_recipients (
+                message_id INTEGER,
+                agent_id INTEGER,
+                kind TEXT,
+                read_ts TEXT,
+                ack_ts TEXT
+            );
+            """
+        )
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO projects (id, slug, human_key) VALUES (1, 'target', '/repo/target')")
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO projects (id, slug, human_key) VALUES (2, 'source', '/repo/source')")
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO agents (id, project_id, name) VALUES (1, 1, 'BlueLake')")
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO agents (id, project_id, name) VALUES (2, 2, 'BlueLake')")
+        conn.execute(
+            """
+            INSERT INTO messages (id, project_id, sender_id, thread_id, subject, body_md, importance, ack_required, created_ts, attachments)
+            VALUES (1, 1, 2, 'cross-thread', 'Cross Project', 'Body', 'high', 0, '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', '[]')
+            """
+        )
+        conn.execute(
+            "INSERT INTO message_recipients (message_id, agent_id, kind, read_ts, ack_ts) VALUES (1, 1, 'to', NULL, NULL)"
+        )
+        conn.commit()
+    finally:
+        conn.close()
+
+
+def test_build_materialized_views_supports_legacy_fts_without_sender_id(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    snapshot = tmp_path / "legacy_fts.sqlite3"
+    conn = sqlite3.connect(snapshot)
+    try:
+        conn.executescript(
+            """
+            CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT, human_key TEXT);
+            CREATE TABLE agents (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                project_id INTEGER,
+                name TEXT,
+                contact_policy TEXT DEFAULT 'auto'
+            );
+            CREATE TABLE messages (
+                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
+                project_id INTEGER,
+                thread_id TEXT,
+                subject TEXT,
+                body_md TEXT,
+                importance TEXT,
+                ack_required INTEGER,
+                created_ts TEXT,
+                attachments TEXT
+            );
+            CREATE TABLE message_recipients (
+                message_id INTEGER,
+                agent_id INTEGER,
+                kind TEXT,
+                read_ts TEXT,
+                ack_ts TEXT
+            );
+            CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE fts_messages USING fts5(subject, body_md);
+            """
+        )
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO projects (id, slug, human_key) VALUES (1, 'legacy', '/repo/legacy')")
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO agents (id, project_id, name) VALUES (1, 1, 'BlueLake')")
+        conn.execute(
+            """
+            INSERT INTO messages (id, project_id, thread_id, subject, body_md, importance, ack_required, created_ts, attachments)
+            VALUES (1, 1, 'legacy-thread', 'Legacy', 'Legacy body', 'normal', 0, '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', '[]')
+            """
+        )
+        conn.execute("INSERT INTO fts_messages (subject, body_md) VALUES ('Legacy', 'Legacy body')")
+        conn.commit()
+    finally:
+        conn.close()
+
+    build_materialized_views(snapshot)
+
+    conn = sqlite3.connect(snapshot)
+    try:
+        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
+        row = conn.execute(
+            """
+            SELECT sender_name, sender_display, sender_project_slug, sender_address
+            FROM fts_search_overview_mv
+            WHERE id = 1
+            """
+        ).fetchone()
+        assert row is not None
+        assert row["sender_name"] == ""
+        assert row["sender_display"] == ""
+        assert row["sender_project_slug"] is None
+        assert row["sender_address"] is None
+    finally:
+        conn.close()
+
+    build_materialized_views(snapshot)
+
+    conn = sqlite3.connect(snapshot)
+    try:
+        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
+        row = conn.execute(
+            """
+            SELECT sender_name, sender_display, sender_project_id, sender_project_slug,
+                   sender_project_name, sender_address
+            FROM message_overview_mv
+            WHERE id = 1
+            """
+        ).fetchone()
+        assert row is not None
+        assert row["sender_name"] == "BlueLake"
+        assert row["sender_display"] == "BlueLake@source"
+        assert row["sender_project_id"] == 2
+        assert row["sender_project_slug"] == "source"
+        assert row["sender_project_name"] == "/repo/source"
+        assert row["sender_address"] == "project:source#BlueLake"
+    finally:
+        conn.close()
